reasons: Khaleed can start fights with Raging Sandstorm, giving Pharsa a clear ranged burst window.
Pharsa can follow Khaleed's engage from safe distance while Khaleed handles frontline initiation.

proof.summary: Khaleed creates the engage or stun window, while Pharsa follows from range with burst damage.
worksBestWhen: Khaleed engages inside Pharsa's follow-up range.
Pharsa waits for Khaleed's real commit before using her main burst window.
failureCases: Khaleed enters too far from Pharsa.
Pharsa is forced to reposition before follow-up damage lands.
The enemy spreads out before Pharsa can punish the engage.

reasons: Luo Yi can create rotation or flank entry with Diversion, helping Khaleed find better Raging Sandstorm angles.
Khaleed's engage can hold enemies in a fight long enough for Luo Yi to apply Yin/Yang reaction pressure.

proof.summary: Luo Yi enables a stronger entry angle, while Khaleed uses that position to start a fight with Raging Sandstorm.
worksBestWhen: The team coordinates Diversion timing before Khaleed commits.
Khaleed enters from fog or flank instead of walking through predictable front vision.
failureCases: Khaleed enters before Luo Yi and teammates are ready.
Luo Yi has no follow-up skills available after the entry.

reasons: Khaleed can create early skirmish pressure and CC windows that help Brody apply Abyss Marks safely.
Brody gives Khaleed's engage a strong marksman damage follow-up during small fights and objective contests.

proof.summary: Khaleed starts or disrupts the fight, while Brody uses the opening to apply Abyss Marks and threaten Torn-Apart Memory burst.
worksBestWhen: Khaleed controls the target inside Brody's attack range.
Brody has enough space to stack marks before finishing.
failureCases: Brody is zoned before he can apply marks.
The enemy uses hard dive onto Brody after Khaleed commits.

reasons: Khaleed can frontline and disrupt enemies, giving Cecilion more safe time to cast from distance.
Cecilion gives Khaleed's engage a ranged magic follow-up that can punish enemies after the initial stun window.

proof.summary: Khaleed creates space in front, while Cecilion follows from range with scaling magic damage.
worksBestWhen: Khaleed starts fights where Cecilion can safely cast.
Cecilion keeps distance behind Khaleed instead of walking into melee range.
failureCases: Khaleed dives too far away from Cecilion.
Cecilion is pressured before the fight starts.
The fight ends too quickly before Cecilion's repeated casting matters.

reasons: Khaleed can force enemies to react to his engage, giving Ixia a better angle for Full Barrage.
Ixia provides AoE damage follow-up while Khaleed handles frontline disruption.

proof.summary: Khaleed starts or disrupts the fight, while Ixia follows if her Full Barrage angle is already prepared.
worksBestWhen: Khaleed engages toward Ixia's firing direction.
Ixia has a safe position before Khaleed commits.
failureCases: Khaleed pushes enemies away from Ixia's damage angle.
Ixia is forced to move and loses her damage window.
The enemy dives Ixia while Khaleed is already committed.
